control_awareness: The amount-based approval control (P2P-C-001) enforces approval levels per request amount but does not aggregate multiple related requests by the same requester/vendor/project. This gap could allow circumvention of higher-level approvals by artificially splitting requests, increasing risk of insufficient oversight and control evasion under delivery deadline pressure.
expected_control_effect: The purchase requests may be approved more quickly individually, but the total combined amount exceeds the department_head threshold, potentially bypassing intended higher-level scrutiny and aggregation controls.
overall_reason: By splitting the purchase into smaller requests below the higher approval threshold, the requester aims to reduce overall approval lead time since manager-level approvals are faster and easier to obtain than department head approvals, thereby meeting the tight customer delivery deadline.
reason: By splitting the purchase into smaller requests below the higher approval threshold, the requester aims to reduce overall approval lead time since manager-level approvals are faster and easier to obtain than department head approvals, thereby meeting the tight customer delivery deadline.
